State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

March 24, 1977


Mr. William L. Shaw
3701 College Avenue
Sacramento, California 95818

Dear Mr. Shaw:

Enclosed please find the original of the materials you submitted to
the Commission concerning the request for an exemption from the requirement
of filing a Conflict of Interest Code by the Sir Francis Drake Commission.
We have copied these materials and made them a part of the record.

We have tentatively scheduled a second hearing of the matter for our
mid-April meeting, April 19, 20, and 21. We will, of course, notify you
of the exact date and time.

With respect to the issues raised in paragraphs 3 and 4 of your letter
of 19 March, I would point out that the proceeding on the question of an
exemption is basically an informal one not subject to the rules of evidence,
etcetera. You will remember that the proceeding was transcribed in its
entirety and a copy can be made available for use in any subsequent court
proceeding.

Mr. Blum has been informed of both the date and time of these hearings
and the nature of the proceeding.

Sincerely,

Kenneth W. Goshorn
Research Specialist III
Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfn
Enclosures

WILLIAM L. SHAW
3701 College Avenue
Sacramento, California 95818
March 25th 1977

Hon. Michael Bennett
Executive Director, FPPC
1100 "K" Street, 3d Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Sir Francis Drake Comm. of California (DC)

Dear Mr. Bennett:

May this respectfully acknowledge and thank
you for a telephone communication received by
the undersigned on or about March 23rd 1977 from
you. You stated in effect that future correspondence
concerning the DC should be directed to you and
that, as I understood it, you hereafter would
represent your agency in my dealings or those of
my attorney with FPPC. I thanked you and assured
you such an arrangement was satisfactory.

In likely unawareness of our understanding, I am
in receipt today of a letter and enclosures posted
March 25, 1977 by your Mr. Goshorn. A copy of the
letter is attached for your ready reference. The
enclosures seem complete in all respects.

Would you kindly acquaint Mr. Goshorn that you
are representing FPPC in regard to any involvement
with or by the undersigned? Thank you sincerely.

Attached for your retention is a copy of each of the
following materials. It will be recalled that on
March fifteenth, Commissioner Ca rpenter inquired
of the undersigned whether there was compliance by
DC with Sections 11 and 12 of the Drake statute;
in other words, do all gift funds to DC or other
owners etc. proceed through the state
Fiscal System? The answer is in the affirmative.
Note the herein attachments:

a. Creation of numbered account 542-04--720-002-00
by the State Controller per Mr. W. C. Chan on
February 27, 1975 for the DC.

b. Form Aud-40, 11/71 to DC, dated March 12, 1976,
From State Controller Div. of Audits. Upon

(To Hon. M. Bennett, FPPC, from Wm. L. Shaw, 3/26/77, p. 2)

the form when received was the written comment in
the lower left corner "Accounted for by General
Service - Larry (?) Wong".

c. Dept. of General Services data sheet entitled
"Don'ts for New Agencies".

The undersigned was accompanied to the Controller's Office
on February 27, 1976 by Mr. Harold Hartsough of DC in order
that he, Mr. Hartsough, newly elected Treasurer of DC might
participate in any discussions in the interest of
learning applicable state fiscal procedure anent the DC.
I do not know system has been followed since on or about
December 4th 1975 when letter notice of acceptance of my
resignation from DC was accepted.

Mr. Goshorn's letter (above cited) mentions Mr. Blum.
I urge you to consider also having present the present
President of DC, namely, Dr. Norman Thrower (Professor
of Geography at UCLA), 751 Swarthmore, Pacific Palisades,
Calif., tel 213-454-5949 (home) or 213-825-3777 or 1071
(office). It is my impression that faculty members of
the University of California are allowed travel funds when
required to travel on state business.

Thank you for consideration of these matters. May I assume
that you will advise Mr. Goshorn that you and not he are now
dealing with Shaw and/or Shaw's attorney identified below.
With all expressions of esteem, I am

[Signature]
William L. Shaw
Tel. (916) 442-7426

encls.
Controller Notice 2/27/75 to DC
"        Form And-4, 3/12/75
Dent. Gen. Services List of Don'ts.

c.o.
Chester J. Rakus
Attorney at Law
1617 - 16th Street
Sacramento, Calif. 95814
Tel. (916) 443-7769

FORM AUG 10 REV J-7TI
STATE CONTROLLER

REQUEST TO STATE CONTROLLER TO ESTABLISH SPECIAL DEPOSIT FUND ACCOUNT

SUBMIT IN QUADRUPLICATE TO CONTROLLER'S DIVISION OF ACCOUNTING, WITH 2 COPIES OF DEPARTMENT OF FINANCE APPROVAL, IF
REQUIRED AND 2 COPIES OF INSTRUMENT OF GIFT OR TRUST WHEN ACCOUNT HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED. AN ACKNOWLEDGED COPY WILL BE
RETURNED TO THE AGENCY AFTER WHICH THE SPECIAL DEPOSIT MAY BE SUBMITTED.

UNLESS EXPRESSLY BY LAW EXPENDITURES FROM THIS ACCOUNT ARE SUBJECT TO GENERAL STATE LAWS GOVERNING THE EXPENDITURE OF
STATE FUNDS (SEE PART 3 SEC 39 GOV. CODE). THIS INCLUDES CONTRACT PURCHASE AND CIVIL SERVICE REQUIREMENTS, BOARD OF
CONTROL RULES APPROVAL BY DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES AND ATTORNEY GENERAL ETC.

CLAIM SCHEDULE WILL SHOW APPROPRIATION AS GOVERNMENT CODE !8370 AND THE ACCOUNT TITLE.

UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF GOV. CODE SECTION 16370 & STATE ADM. MANUAL SEC. 18420,
PLEASE ESTABLISH THE FOLLOWING SPECIAL DEPOSIT FUND ACCOUNT.

PROPOSED ACCOUNT TITLE

SIR FRANCIS DRAKE COMMISSION CH 330/71

SOURCE OF MONIES

DONATIONS AND GRANTS

PURPOSE OF ACCOUNT:

TO ACCOUNT FOR DONATIONS AND GRANTS

DATE OF TRUST INSTRUMENT | DATE OF D.O.F APPROVAL, IF REQUIRED BY 11405 OR 11405.1 GOV. CODE OR OTHER LAW

DEPOSIT OR USE OF BALANCE | TO OTHER EDUCATIONAL PROGRAM | STATUTE REFERENCE, IF ANY
                                                        | CH 530/71

(If expenditure authorized by instrument to be completed by agency submit no claim schedule)
                                                        | 
PLEASE GIVE A BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE NATURE OF EXPENDITURES TO BE INCURRED, AND IF APPROPRIATE, THE PROJECT
TO BE COMPLETED ETC OR ATTACH DEPARTMENT DATA:

Plan and execute suitable commemorative activities for international celebration
of the 400th Anniversary of Sir Francis Drake's historic circumnavigation of the earth
in the years 1577 to 1580.

PERIOD OF AVAILABLE:

Without Regard to Fiscal Years

DEPARTMENT | SIR FRANCIS DRAKE COMMISSION ( S.F.D.C.) | DATE | February 27, 1975

SIGNED | [illegible signature and text] | TITLE | President S.F.D.C.

DO NOT COMPLETE BELOW HEAVY LINE

[REMARKS - INSTRUCTIONS TO CLERK & AUDITORS]

ACCOUNT ESTAB SHLD | DATE | February 27, 1975 | AUDIT FILE ESTABLISHD

ACCOUNT NUMBER | 942-940-720-002-00

[illegible signature marks]

WILLIAM L. SHAW
3701 College Avenue
Sacramento, California 95818
March 19th 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807, 1100 "M" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention Kenneth M. Goshorn, Esq.
Conflicts of Interest Division

Re: Sir Francis Drake Commission of California (DC)

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

Thank you for the telephone call to the Undersigned
on March 17th 1977, 1710 hours. I regret that you were
pressed for time as the first (five and one half) minutes
allowed by you did not permit me to go into several
matters which seemed pertinente to the FPPC sessions
concerning DC.

Additional items are:

1. In any resetting, please avoid Wednesday, March 30th
1977, between the hours 1100 and 1500. The reason is
a vital prior commitment involving numerous other
persons.

2. Kindly return to the undersigned, the memorandum as to Mr.
Blum and the exhibits or pertinently forty-three (23)
in number. You will recall that you in effect borrowed
the same in order to make a copy of each separate item.
Doubtless your counsel necessarily has finished the duplicating
process. I again concur in your comment to his Gordon
who in response to her request for a copy of everything
submitted before informed by you that she could read your
copy of each item.

3. Challenge is reared as to the legality of the presence
of Mr. John Knox or any representative of the gentlemen
at any hearing concerned with the DC, in any participating
capacity. As you are aware, legislators are entitled
to state commissions, but, invariably, such appointees
represent the House to which they are elected. Mr. Knox
of the Assembly, at all times here involved, attempts
to represent the State Senate by an appointment of the
Senate Committee on Rules! It is submitted that Mr. Knox
has never had a bond fide status or the DC and is thus
an interloper. Evidently this is a general consensus.

(To PPFC, No. Governor, From Wm. L. Shaw, 3/19/77, p. 2)

I should welcome the receipt of your citations
within the Legislature may legally represent
upon a state commission, a House of the Legislature
to which he has never been elected. Further,
may not a councilman be a pointed by the Senate
Judiciary Committee and the issue of representation
be left silent.

4. The letter narrative by Dr. Thrower, December
22, 1976, to PPFC per you is unsworn. Can
you knowingly build up a record in advance
containing significant unsworn statements
where the record is subject to judicial review
(made in the Superior Court) based upon such
casual, unsworn matter as the Thrower letter
contains? Must you not create a sound record
containing sworn matter (a distinct from
letters or notes or memos which when drafted
were not to be knowingly used in court)?
I should release your authorities upon this
feature.

5. Just for the record have you come into the
Blum involvement with Mr. Arthur L. Blum?
If the answer is in the affirmative did you
true or otherwise memorialize your discussions?

Thank you for your consideration. With all expressions of
esteem, I am

Sincerely

[signature]
William L. Shaw
Tel. 442-7426

C.V.
Chester J. Rekus
Attorney at Law
1617 - 16th Street
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

WILLIAM L. SHAW
3701 College Avenue
Sacramento, California 95818
March 17th 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 207, 1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention Kenneth W. Goshorn, Esq.
Conflicts of Interest Division

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

This will acknowledge and thank you for your
memorandum and two (2) attached enclosures
received by the undersigned in course of mail
on March 16th 1977.

I have sought to reach you by telephone calls to
your office and by personal calls at your office,
1100 "K" Street, 4th Floor, but the results have
not been affirmative. I am told that you emerged
in many and diverse matters.

The purpose of this letter is to disclose to you
exactly what I am seeking from the FPPC. This was
mentioned briefly to the Commission on or about
March 15th 1977. Further two (2) documents within
the materials entrusted to you at that time bear
upon the issue.

In early 1975 when I was President of the Drake
Commission, I sought and obtained from each of the
Control IRB and from the California Franchise Tax
Board, a letter ruling that the Drake Commission
(hereinafter DC) was tax exempt as a non-profit
organization. Each separate agency questioned me
at their respective offices and stressed their subse-
quent rulings upon my assurances that no member of
DC or any other party received any "profit"
or dividends from membership or association with DC.
A copy of each agency ruling is within the file now
resting with you.

I sincerely believed that I correctly stated the
no profit (or private) matter to each of the nation
and state governments. Others on DC shared my belief.

By letter, dated June 19, 1975, from the Plymouth (England)
Lord Mayor, the DC was advised that D.R. Blum had undertaken
for the benefit of a "feasibility study" there was
no mention of any compensation past or future. This letter is
within the file in your possession.

When I questioned Mr. Blum as to the nature and meaning
of a feasibility study, he explained orally that he was
studying and recommending that Plymouth celebrate on streets
the years from 1977 to 1980; he recorded that attention
not be given only to the year 1980.

When I later asked what Mr. Blum was receiving as compensation
or reward from Plymouth about the feasibility study, he (Mr.
Blum) declined to answer.

By letter, May 28th 1975 (now within the same file resting
with you) Mr. Blum characterized himself as "the guest of
the Plymouth City Council in my capacity as a public relations
counsel - - - -". The word "guest" is misleading and led the
undersigned to believe that Mr. Blum was not an employee or
hired agent or representative of Plymouth.

It is now definitely established that Mr. Blum has received
substantial compensation paid to him by Plymouth.

QUERY: What is the effect of the tardy revelations by Mr.
Blum as to his private profit involving DC? Did not Shaw
although in good faith misrepresent to IRS and to Franchise
Tax Board that no members or others received any profit or
gain directly or indirectly from involvement with DC?

This issue is that I have sought to bring before your
honorable commission. I regret that you were unable to
reach by phone, call at your office, phone to your home,
etc.. After sending me your memo March 9th 1977, with
enclosures, it was reasonable to assume you might answer
the phone, etc. I am most appreciative to those others
in your office who listened to me, made notes, and promised
to bring the matter to your attention.

Sincerely

[signature]
William L. Shaw
Tel. 442-7426

C.O.
Chester J. Hakus
Attorney at Law
1617 - 16th Street
Sacramento, Calif. 95814
